summary.overview:
- **Survey Launch:** Test survey launched today at 9:00 AM Phoenix time, with 45 participants across multiple locations.  
- **Reminder Strategy:** Multi-channel reminders: SMS on January 15, WhatsApp on January 17, email on January 19.  
- **Translation Updates:** Automatic translations in French, Spanish, and Haitian Creole; manual corrections based on feedback.  
- **Data Security:** Survey access requires employee ID or email validation; no OTP for anonymity.  
- **Reporting Demo:** Demo scheduled for next week to showcase reporting capabilities and secure access controls.  
- **Feedback Integration:** Test survey will guide final updates and improvements; follow-up meeting next Wednesday to assess progress.
summary.action_items:
**Karthik Rao**
Publish the test survey at 9 AM Phoenix time immediately after the call (07:52)
Send the generic survey link and QR code to the team for access testing and feedback collection (13:34)
Monitor and address any survey issues reported by the team; serve as primary contact for support (21:09)
Coordinate with engineering and Sabrish to develop multilingual launch email templates based on data from Rob and ensure translations are ready (17:18)
Prepare and deliver a demo session on reporting features focusing on manager and leadership views for the next week’s call (19:38)

**Carol Meichner-Smith**
Share HR director contact details and ensure all 45 participants are included in the platform data upload (06:00)
Coordinate internally to confirm language preferences and finalize the list of participants requiring translated launch emails (18:00)
Lead testing of generic link and QR code accessibility with team and additional participants, including testing for unauthorized access prevention (22:09)
Provide feedback on the test survey and translation accuracy to Karthik promptly during the survey window (19:13)

**Elaina Skala**
Test survey using home phone numbers for text/WhatsApp reminders and provide feedback on reminder reception and survey access (07:15)
Support testing QR code and generic link functionality, including conducting unauthorized access tests with non-pilot members (22:57)

**Christine Belyeu**
Evaluate survey reminder effectiveness and timing, especially with regard to internal group response planning (11:37)
Participate in testing of survey links and reminder messaging to ensure functionality (22:05)
summary.notes:
## **Survey Launch and Participant Management**

The team finalized launching the **test survey** today at **9:00 AM Phoenix time** to validate workflow, translations, and participant experience (07:49).

- **Karthik Rao confirmed the test survey launch** with **45 participants**, including HR directors, ensuring employee data accuracy for all (05:34).
    - Participants cover multiple locations with **multilingual support enabled in French, Spanish, and Haitian Creole** for survey questions and landing pages.
    - The test survey duplicates the final survey setup to allow updates after feedback, mainly focusing on translation accuracy and question order.
    - The survey will remain open until **Tuesday, January 20**, closing at midnight to maximize participation.
    - PTO absence of one participant was addressed by removing them from the list, with flexibility to add later if needed (06:27).

- **Launch timing was optimized** to accommodate time zones, starting early post-call at 9:00 AM local time to reach West Coast participants before the East Coast workday peak (07:49).

- **Multiple reminder channels will be tested**: SMS on **January 15**, WhatsApp on **January 17**, and email on **January 19**, aiming to maximize response rates through preferred communication methods (09:36).
    - SMS reminders will go to all participants with phone numbers, WhatsApp only to those with verified WhatsApp contacts.
    - Email reminders include dynamic placeholders for employee name, survey end date, and unique links.
    - Elaina Skala and Carol Meichner-Smith emphasized testing on language preferences and internal coordination to exclude any participants who should not receive reminders (11:37).

- **Survey access via unique links, generic URLs, and QR codes** was enabled to simplify entry and test user preference between employee ID or email address for validation (13:34).
    - Karthik recommended avoiding verification codes to maintain anonymity and reduce user friction.
    - Carol and team will test whether email or employee ID is more user-friendly to finalize the validation method for the main survey.
    - Access permissions are enforced so only authorized participants can submit responses, preventing unauthorized access (23:16).

## **Multilingual and Translation Enhancements**

The team is actively addressing **multilingual support and translation quality** to ensure accurate and culturally appropriate survey content (04:16).

- **Automatic translations for French, Spanish, and Haitian Creole are deployed** but manual updates will be made based on tester feedback to fix errors or improve clarity (05:34).
- Karthik and engineering, including product manager Sabrish, are working on adding **multilingual email templates** for launch and reminders, pending approval of language preference data from Rob’s team (17:18).
- Carol proposed a workaround to **manually update language preferences at the property level** if Rob’s data set does not include them in time, requiring a lead time of about **one week** for data preparation and upload (17:49).
- The team agreed translations for emails are a “nice to have” but not critical, prioritizing survey question translations first (18:38).

## **Survey Data Integrity and Anonymity**

Maintaining **data integrity and participant anonymity** was a key concern addressed during the meeting (14:00).

- Survey access requires either employee ID or email validation to match participant data securely while preserving anonymity (14:00).
- Karthik advised against using OTP-based verification codes as it could undermine anonymity perception among participants (15:26).
- Carol expects email addresses will be used for internal club surveys, but employee IDs may be preferred for the larger quarterly survey to reduce email exposure (15:38).
- The system restricts access if unauthorized employees attempt to access the survey via QR code or link, ensuring data security and accuracy (23:16).

## **Reporting and Leadership Access Planning**

The next phase involves **demonstrating reporting capabilities** with a focus on manager and leadership access control to protect sensitive data (19:13).

- Karthik plans to present a **reporting demo next week** showing how managers and leaders will access survey results with role-based permissions limiting data visibility (19:13).
- Leadership will receive access only to their company’s data, not full admin rights, to maintain security and confidentiality.
- Carol noted leadership meetings to review survey results are scheduled after the follow-up call, enabling incorporation of feedback from the test survey into reporting demos (20:30).

## **Support and Communication Protocol**

Clear communication channels and support protocols were established to address any survey issues promptly (21:09).

- Karthik requested all survey-related problems be sent directly to him via email to ensure swift response and resolution (21:26).
- Carol, Elaina, and Christine confirmed readiness to escalate issues and coordinate internally if survey participants face difficulties.
- The team agreed that the generic links and QR codes are now accessible for testing, with a brief delay expected before full activation post-launch (22:12).
- Testing confirmed that early survey access attempts show a “not ready” message until the official launch, preventing premature submissions (23:58).

## **Process Validation and Feedback Integration**

The team is focused on **iterative testing and feedback collection** to refine the survey experience ahead of the final release (00:00–24:44).

- The test survey acts as a live environment for participants and admin users to uncover any UI, translation, or data issues before the main launch.
- Carol and Elaina will lead initial testing, with a small group held back to verify reminder functionality on SMS and WhatsApp later in the cycle (12:22).
- Feedback from this test will inform question order, language updates, template improvements, and final participant access methods.
- Karthik and Sabrish will coordinate engineering updates on requested features such as hiding survey completion time and adding multilingual email support.
- Follow-up meetings are scheduled for next Wednesday to review progress and incorporate test results into the broader rollout plan (24:05).
